stemHtml:
During a training flight at low altitudes, an instructor simulates an engine failure and notices that the student pilot is becoming very nervous and is acting too slowly. Therefore, the instructor takes over the controls. After landing the student pilot feels weak and is over- breathing.
The instructor tells him/her to breathe slowly and to talk about the flight because the student pilot is:

options.a: Suffering from hypoxia, but his/her poor performance must be immediately discussed.
options.b: Hyperventilating; speaking aloud helps to increase the oxygen exchange rate and restore normal respiration.
options.c: Suffering from hypoxia; speaking aloud allows for deeper breaths and helps to restore normal levels of blood CO<sub>2</sub>.
options.d: Hyperventilating; speaking aloud and breathing slowly are good countermeasures.
